quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: Hungarian paprika isn't just a spice—it's a national treasure! Hungary has eight official grades of paprika, ranging from mild and sweet to fiery hot. The town of Kalocsa is known as the 'Paprika Capital' and celebrates this vibrant red spice with festivals and museums.

short_read.title: The Secret Lives of Harbor Seals in the Pacific Northwest
short_read.teaser: Harbor seals are year-round neighbors in the Strait of Juan de Fuca, and they're full of surprises. Learn about their playful personalities and how mothers recognize their pups by their unique voices.

medium_read.title: Why July is the Perfect Time for Garden Maintenance
medium_read.teaser: Mid-summer brings its own joys and challenges to Pacific Northwest gardens. This guide shares gentle tips for deadheading, watering wisely, and enjoying the fruits of your spring labor.

long_read.title: The Grand Tour: How Travel Shaped the Modern World
long_read.teaser: For centuries, travelers journeyed across Europe seeking art, culture, and enlightenment. This fascinating history explores how the tradition of travel transformed both individuals and societies.

port_angeles.title: Butterfly Season at Hurricane Ridge
port_angeles.description: July is peak butterfly season at Hurricane Ridge! On sunny days, you can spot dozens of species including the beautiful Olympic Marble butterfly, found nowhere else on Earth. The wildflower meadows are alive with color and gentle wings right now.

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's heart beats approximately ___ times per minute during flight.
puzzle_1.correct_response: Amazing! Yes, 1,200 beats per minute—that tiny heart works incredibly hard to power those magical hovering wings!
puzzle_1.close_response: Very close! A hummingbird's heart beats about 1,200 times per minute—that's 20 times per second!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: Those tiny hearts are mighty! A hummingbird's heart beats about 1,200 times per minute during flight—imagine the energy that takes!
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: To fuel this incredible metabolism, hummingbirds must visit hundreds of flowers each day and can consume up to twice their body weight in nectar daily. Their hearts slow to about 250 beats per minute when they sleep at night.
puzzle_1.learn_more_label: Learn more — Cornell Lab of Ornithology

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'butterfly'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Wonderful! Pillangó is indeed butterfly—such a lovely, light word for such a delicate creature!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The Hungarian word for butterfly is 'pillangó'—doesn't it sound as light and graceful as the creature itself?
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: The word 'pillangó' comes from an old Hungarian word meaning 'to flutter.' Other choices were madár (bird), virág (flower), and méh (bee)—all beautiful parts of a summer garden!

puzzle_3.question: What type of tree produces acorns?
puzzle_3.correct_response: Perfect! Oak trees produce acorns, and they're vital food for squirrels, deer, and many birds—a single oak can produce thousands of acorns in a good year!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: It's the oak tree that produces acorns! These nutritious nuts feed countless animals, and Native peoples of the Pacific Northwest traditionally used them to make flour and other foods.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: The Olympic Peninsula is home to the majestic Oregon white oak, which produces sweet acorns that were an important food source for local indigenous communities. These trees can live for hundreds of years and support entire ecosystems.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
